academic_requirements.summary: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent foreign qualification. The programme requires proven preparation in Information Technology and Information Engineering; for applicants with an Italian degree, the official programme site specifies at least 42 ECTS split across computing and mathematics sectors, plus an adequate final degree grade. Foreign degrees are evaluated by the Admission Committee together with the official country-based entry-title and GPA rules.
academic_requirements.prior_degree_or_diploma: Bachelor's degree or equivalent foreign qualification.
academic_requirements.subject_requirements: The official programme site states that applicants with an Italian degree must have at least 42 ECTS, including 24 ECTS in SSD INF/01 - ING-INF/05 - NG/INF-03 and 18 ECTS in SSD MAT/01 - MAT/09. The apply.unipd.it course page also requires proven skills in Information Technology and Information Engineering, such as the basics of programming, communication networks, and information systems.
academic_requirements.gpa_or_country_requirements: For applicants with an Italian degree, personal preparation includes a degree grade greater than or equal to 84/110 or equivalent. Applicants with foreign degrees are evaluated by the Admission Committee, and the University also applies country-based specific entry-title and GPA requirements on the official application platform.

language_requirements.required_level: English B2 (CEFR) or equivalent; the programme site also states at least B2 in reading and listening.
accepted_proofs: IELTS Academic or General Training: minimum score 6.0.
Cambridge English B2 First: minimum score 173.
Trinity College London Integrated Skills in English ISE II.
Oxford Test of English: minimum score 126.
GATEHOUSE ESOL International Classic B2: GA Level 1 Certificate.
Pearson Test of English General: PTE General 3.
Pearson English International Certificate: Level 3 Upper Intermediate.
Pearson Test of English Academic: minimum score 65.
exemption_rules: Applicants are exempt if their previous education leading to the entry qualification was completed entirely in English.
Applicants are exempt if they are native English speakers from one of the countries listed by the University of Padua.

application_deadline_eu.notes: The third call is only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

application_deadline_non_eu.call_three: Not applicable for non-EU applicants residing outside Italy.
application_deadline_non_eu.notes: The dedicated programme site states that non-EU students residing abroad have 50 places, 10 of which are reserved for the Marco Polo Programme; the third call is restricted to EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy.

required_documents.notes: Upload a colour scanned PDF; an English or Italian translation must be provided if needed.

required_documents.document_name: Academic qualification or provisional enrolment certificate
required_documents.notes: If the final qualification has not yet been awarded, an enrolment or provisional certificate is accepted.
EU citizens and non-EU citizens residing in Italy may use an ID card; other applicants normally provide a passport.
Required only for non-EU applicants residing in Italy.

required_documents.document_name: English language certificate or other accepted proof
required_documents.notes: Required unless the applicant qualifies for an exemption under the University language rules.

required_documents.document_name: Original and legalised bachelor's degree with Italian or English translation
required_documents.notes: Required after admission to complete enrolment.

required_documents.document_name: Original and legalised Transcript of Records with Italian or English translation
required_documents.notes: The transcript must be issued by the competent academic authority and show passed exams and grades.

required_documents.document_name: Universitaly pre-enrolment and study visa D - enrolment
required_documents.notes: Non-EU citizens residing outside Italy must pre-enrol through Universitaly no later than 15 July and then obtain a study visa.

required_documents.document_name: Residence permit or residence-permit application receipt
required_documents.notes: Required for non-EU applicants at enrolment if they are in Italy.

entry_exam_or_test.test_name: No standard written entry test; admission committee evaluation of curriculum and personal preparation
entry_exam_or_test.dates: No fixed written-test or interview calendar is published.
entry_exam_or_test.scoring_or_threshold: Assessment is based on curricular requirements, degree background, and personal preparation. For Italian-degree applicants, the official programme site specifies at least 42 ECTS in the listed sectors and a final degree grade of at least 84/110 or equivalent; the programme site also requires English at least B2 in reading and listening.

source_quotes.quote: 24 ECTS credits achieved in SSD INF/01 – ING-INF/05 – NG/INF-03;
18 ECTS credits achieved in SSD MAT/01 – MAT/09.
Greater than or equal to 84/110 or equivalent.
Call Three: 2 June 2026 – 2 August 2026 (only for EU students and non-EU students residing in Italy)

uncertainty_notes: The programme appears under both Engineering and Science in central English master's listings, but the official course pages and dedicated programme site describe one Cybersecurity master's degree; this record treats it as a single programme.
